flagsFinalPass.label: Flags final pass
flagsFinalPass.note: Finish the field with flag markers and final visibility checks once the main numbered obstacle line is in place.

conesTrackWalk.label: Cone placement during track walk (optional)
conesTrackWalk.note: Cones can be placed during the pilot track walk to avoid a separate pass. If you prefer to set them earlier, allow a little extra time on site.

crewUnloadStage.label: Unload and stage equipment
crewUnloadStage.note: Unload the vehicle, sort anchors and hardware, and lay out the kit so frame prep does not block field placement later.

crewPreassemble.label: Pre-assemble frames and soft goods
crewPreassemble.noteWithSoftGoods: Prep PVC gate frames and beach flags before walking items onto the field so the placement pass can stay focused on spacing.
crewPreassemble.noteWithoutSoftGoods: Prep PVC gate frames before walking items onto the field so the placement pass can stay focused on spacing.

crewRiggingCheck.label: Rigging and anchor check
crewRiggingCheck.note: Confirm anchor points, overhead lines, and the clearance plan before lifting or suspending heavier structures.

obstacleNoteWithNumber: {note} Use obstacle #{number} on the map as the placement reference.
summaryHeavy: {count, plural, one {1 higher-effort structure should be placed and secured before the standard gate pass.} other {{count} higher-effort structures should be placed and secured before the standard gate pass.}} The timing assumes a typical 2-3 person crew and may increase when overhead rigging or long carry distances are involved.
summaryLight: Most of the setup time comes from unloading, pre-assembling frames, and then placing the numbered obstacle line cleanly with a 2-3 person crew.
crewAssumption: Timing is based on a typical 2-3 person crew and assumes standard club-style prep: unload, stage, pre-assemble, then place and secure.

obstacleNotes.gate: Place and square this gate once the anchor structures are in place.
obstacleNotes.tower: Place and secure this with the other higher-effort structures. Towers need stable feet, clean vertical alignment, and enough clearance for stacked openings.
obstacleNotes.flag: Use flags for final visibility and edge definition after the main obstacles are in place.
obstacleNotes.cone: Use cones in the final cleanup pass for boundary marking and spacing checks.
obstacleNotes.label: No setup guidance is available for this item yet.
obstacleNotes.polyline: No setup guidance is available for this item yet.
obstacleNotes.startfinish: Set this first so the launch and finish reference is fixed before the rest of the course goes down.
obstacleNotes.ladder: Treat this as a higher-effort item. Allow extra time if it needs overhead rigging or a careful suspend point.
obstacleNotes.divegate: Place and secure this early. Dive gates usually need more alignment and anchoring than a standard gate.
obstacleNotes.barrier: Set up barriers after the main gates and obstacles are positioned.
